title: How to spot a scam email
description: Learn to identify email tricks (phishing) used by online scammers to steal your passwords or money.

steps.title: Examine the sender's full email address
steps.body: Do not just look at the sender's name. Click or tap on their name to reveal the full email address. If the name is 'Netflix' but the address is 'netflix-support@gmail-billing-checks.xyz', it is fake.

steps.title: Look for panic or urgency
steps.body: Scammers want you to act without thinking. Watch out for warnings like 'Your bank account will be closed in 24 hours' or 'You must pay this bill immediately'.

steps.title: Check the greeting and spelling
steps.body: Scams often start with generic greetings like 'Dear Customer' or 'Hello User' instead of your name. Look for spelling mistakes and bad grammar.

steps.title: Do not click links or open attachments
steps.body: If you are suspicious, do not click anything. Open a new browser window, search for the official bank or company website, and contact them directly.

glossary.definition: A trick where scammers send fake emails that look like they come from real businesses (like banks) to steal your information.
A file (like a photo or document) sent inside an email that can contain malware if sent by a scammer.
